message:
test(core,e2e): add conformance oracle S76, enable fast-path tests, add append-path E2E

Conformance oracle Session 76 (conformance_oracle_ext.rs):
- s76a: complex UPSERT with WHERE guard
- s76b: INSERT OR REPLACE with triggers
- s76c: 4-level FK CASCADE
- s76d: nested SAVEPOINT/RELEASE/ROLLBACK
- s76e: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with defaults

Fast-path separation tests (fast_path_separation.rs):
- Un-ignore and rename test_query_row_indexed_equality_uses_direct_
  counter and test_query_row_rowid_range_uses_direct_counter (these
  now pass with the new indexed equality + rowid range fast paths)
- Update SELECT column lists from explicit to SELECT *

Superinstruction V2 tests (v2_superinstruction_tests.rs):
- New test_v2_append_path_with_concurrent_mode_on_disjoint_tables:
  2 connections, concurrent mode, 128 rows each into separate tables,
  verifies append path stays hot
- New helpers: new_file_conn(), explicit_row_insert_sql(), v2_test_guard()
- insert_append_hint_clear_count assertions added to sequential and
  midstream tests

Cross-engine compat test (compat_tests.rs):
- parity_sqlite_created_cursor_state_vscdb_reads_with_frankensqlite:
  creates .vscdb with rusqlite (cursorDiskKV + ItemTable), reads with
  FrankenSQLite using parameterized range queries and LIKE patterns

Track T append-path E2E oracle (new file):
- 10K sequential rowid insert comparing FrankenSQLite vs rusqlite,
  asserting append_count >= 9900, seek_count <= 32, and
  append_hint_clear_count <= 4
- Optional throughput probe benchmark (sequential vs gapped patterns)
- Structured JSON artifact emission via FSQLITE_TRACK_T_E2E_ARTIFACT

Minor: for-in-single-element-slice cleanup in conformance_oracle_s55.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
